newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news. Today's -5.68% is flow-driven: ALGM jumped ~6.8% on June 8 in the sector-wide rebound (Jensen Huang 'buy at a discount' comments after the June 4-5 Broadcom-triggered chip rout) and is giving that bounce back as semis fade again. Only minor items: SVP of Worldwide Sales sold 5,000 shares (~$264K, immaterial) and post-earnings analyst PT raises (Jefferies, Evercore ISI, UBS) that trace to the May FQ4 print (revenue $243M +26% y/y, data center revenue quadrupled to 14% of Q4 sales), not to the past three days.

sources.label: StockStory - Allegro MicroSystems and Monolithic Power Systems Shares Are Soaring (June 8 rebound)
CNBC - Broadcom, Micron and ARM sink, leading chip stocks lower (June 4-5 rout context)
TipRanks - Allegro insider sale (SVP Madormo, 5,000 shares)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down move (87 -> 93) already priced the post-79% YTD crowding; today's -5.7% is a flow-driven giveback of June 8's sector bounce with no new fundamentals, which marginally restores asymmetry but does not change ALGM's indirect position vs HBM/packaging/CPO bottleneck names on this page.
verdicts.bull: Data center revenue quadrupled y/y to 14% of Q4 sales and current-sensor/power content scales with rack power density; a 5.7% flow-driven dip on an intact thesis is a slightly better entry.
verdicts.neutral: Still up 69% YTD with the AI-adjacent rerating largely done; rank 93 fairly reflects selective, second-derivative exposure to the compute bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: 71% of sales remain auto (xEV/ADAS) - a cyclical end-market - and the Broadcom-triggered de-rating of AI chip multiples plus smartphone/memory demand warnings can compress analog multiples regardless of ALGM's data-center optionality.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued data center revenue mix shift (quantified again at the next earnings print) or a named AI power-delivery design win.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Auto/industrial cycle softness combined with sector-wide AI multiple compression after the June chip rout.

verdicts.rankRationale: On a CPO-lens page the binding constraint is relevance, not price: ALGM remains mostly auto/industrial with low CPO exposure, so today's flow-driven -5.7% (reversing June 8's +6.8% sector bounce) modestly de-froths the 69% YTD run but does not justify a rank change after yesterday's down move was already applied.
verdicts.bull: Sell-off without news improves entry on a name whose data-center current-sensing business quadrupled y/y; any optics/CPO power-monitoring content disclosure would be a genuine surprise to this list.
verdicts.neutral: Low CPO relevance keeps it appropriately deep in the table; price oscillation within a sector-wide whipsaw is noise for this page's thesis.
verdicts.bear: Crowding risk persists - still +69% YTD, GuruFocus flags overvaluation vs GF Value, and an insider sale at the highs - while CPO-specific catalysts are essentially absent.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any disclosed content win in optical-module or CPO power delivery/sensing; otherwise data-center mix updates at earnings.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Multiple compression on auto-heavy revenue if the chip-sector de-rating extends, with no CPO catalyst to offset it.
